steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects two sentences describing opposite starting points: cartographers begin at the lowest point (the Colville River), while hydrologists begin at the top (the Siksikpuk River). The transition must signal that the second approach goes in the opposite direction from the first.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In a similar way,: Signals similarity — that what follows parallels what came before. But hydrologists start at the top, not the bottom; the two approaches are opposite, not similar. ✗
  • Choice B — For example,: Introduces an illustration of a general point. The second sentence is not an example of cartographers starting at the bottom; it's a contrasting practice. ✗
  • Choice C — By contrast,: Directly signals that the upcoming information opposes the previous information. Hydrologists starting at the top directly contrasts with cartographers starting at the bottom. ✓
  • Choice D — In other words,: Signals a restatement or clarification of the same idea. The second sentence introduces a different group with a different method — it is not a restatement. ✗
That matches Choice C — By contrast,.
